description: URIs present in profile.skills_uri that do NOT reflect an actual skill of the CV author. Each entry traces the surface token that introduced the noise.

noise_items.origin_in_cv: 2022–2024 | Data Analyst — Analytics Corp, Paris  AND  Master Data Science — Université Paris-Saclay
noise_items.why_its_noise: 'Paris' in the CV refers to the city (employer location + university location). The ESCO URI cb668e89... maps to the concept 'paris' in the gambling/betting sense (French: 'paris' = wagers/bets). Cross-domain semantic collision: geographic proper noun matched against an unrelated ESCO label.

noise_items.origin_in_cv: - Methods: Agile, Project management, KPI dashboards, Reporting
noise_items.why_its_noise: CV says 'Project management' (methodology/process). The alias layer maps the isolated token 'management' to 'gérer une équipe' (= manage a team, i.e., people management). The candidate's CV shows no line management / direct reports — title is 'Data Analyst' and 'Junior BI Developer'. The alias collapses a project-management methodology signal onto a people-management URI.

noise_items.origin_in_cv: Created Excel reports and Power BI dashboards for sales and finance teams
noise_items.why_its_noise: The word 'sales' appears as the business unit the Data Analyst reported FOR, not a skill the candidate has. SKILL_ALIASES['sales']=['argumentaire de vente'] triggers indiscriminately on the surface token 'sales' without context awareness. The output implies the candidate does sales pitches, which the CV does not support.

Borderline. SKILL_ALIASES['agile']=['gestion de projets par méthode agile', 'développement par itérations']. The second expansion ('iterative development') is injected even though the CV only declares 'Agile' as a method — not specifically iterative development practice. Double-counts the same source signal as TWO ESCO URIs.

Not noise per se (the candidate IS a Data Analyst), but four distinct surface tokens collapse onto ONE URI — the URI has no signal about the SPECIFIC aspects of data analysis the candidate does (SQL querying, dashboard creation, ML modeling). Dilution of specificity.

noise_overview.noise_ratio_strict: 3 / 17 ≈ 17.6% of final skills_uri are clearly unsupported by the CV

root_causes_summary: Alias expansion in SKILL_ALIASES is context-free: single-word tokens ('sales', 'management', 'data', 'power') trigger on any mention, without regard to whether the surrounding sentence attributes the skill to the candidate.

Alias map (esco_alias_fr_v0.jsonl) at the skill_filter.py layer compounds with SKILL_ALIASES: two independent alias layers, both triggering on single tokens, both mapping to ESCO URIs without semantic gate.
No proper-noun / geographic guard: ESCO index contains labels ('paris' = wager) that collide with city names. No filter rejects geographic tokens before map_skill.
No counter-alias: once a token is expanded into multiple ESCO URIs (e.g. 'agile' -> 2 URIs), all are admitted — no policy chooses 'most specific' vs 'most general'.
